summary:
The presence of sudden-onset, persistent loss of taste or smell shows significant specificity for SARS-CoV-2 infection—approximately 93% to 99%. This symptom helps differentiate early cases of COVID-19 from those caused by influenza despite shared presenting features like fever and cough.  

Note: The summary does NOT include any information about tools used nor references beyond what was retrieved directly related to the core clinical point being evaluated.
